rationale: Contract-stability re-verification of ad56c295 (delta vs r1 68fe576e = internal/config only, +103/-2). No exported surface changed: git diff shows NO added/removed exported func/type/var/const — all resolvers, PanelGateKeys, CountValue, ReviewerSlot, KnownModels are byte-identical to r1. The only production change is two fmt.Errorf recovery clauses inside the UNEXPORTED validateGates, now routing the attacker-controlled gate key and substitutes self-map key through strconv.Quote. Escaping is message-text only: it touches no resolver output, no loaded value, no struct field; the refusal CONDITIONS (len(unknown)>0, k==v) are unchanged, so the load-vs-refuse set is identical. Both reworded recovery lines still satisfy guard.HasFinalRecoveryLine: the added TestLoad_UnknownGateKeyEscapesControlBytes PASSES (gate-key path), and I empirically confirmed the substitutes self-map path — pure error message = exactly 2 physical lines, last line 'recovery: '-prefixed with content, key escaped to "ba\x1b\a\nd", zero raw ESC/BEL bytes. 109 byte-compat identity untouched: TestLoad_GatesAbsentByteIdentical109 PASSES; a 109-accepted config returns nil from validateGates before reaching either line. go build ./... exit 0, full internal/config green.

findings.summary: Delta confirmed escaping + tests only. No regression on the contract lens: exported signatures, PanelGateKeys order, CountValue, ReviewerSlot shape, and the gates-absent 109 identity path are all unchanged by the fix. Both my round-1 low-severity notes (empty-family narrowing edge; ReviewerSlot json tags) are unaffected and remain non-blocking.

Housekeeping for the tally, not a code defect: reviewer S2 left two untracked scratch test files in the package this round (internal/config/zzz_s2_scratch_probe_test.go, zzz_s2_scratch_probe2_test.go), mirroring S1's r1 leak — both violate the BRIEF's 'scratch under /tmp' rule. I did not touch them (READ-ONLY). They must be removed before merge so `go test ./internal/config` stays hermetic. My verdict is unaffected: my targeted -run verifications only exercise the named Bead-1 tests, which pass.
